finding: _draw_line may recompute span block width/boundaries per wrapped display line instead of once per row, making rendering O(lines x cols) for tall/wrapped span rows
proposed_action: Precompute per-row col->(block_width,colspan,is_placeholder) map before the inner per-line loop; index it in the loop

finding: F11 horizontal-rule junction suppression (3.9a) has no complexity bound; naive per-rule full-row scan is O(rows^2 x cols) per draw()
proposed_action: Precompute suppressed vertical boundaries once per draw() in one O(cells) pass; O(1) lookup per boundary; forbid per-rule full-table scan

finding: 3.6 allocates a new VistabCell per cell per render on top of ingestion-time objects -> M x N allocations + attribute copies per render
proposed_action: Cap to one allocation per cell per render or carry lightweight tuples through the render pipeline; no per-line allocation

finding: 3.5/3.1a call _len_cell(str(cell)); repeated str() allocation and split work can run in front of the LRU-cached vislen
proposed_action: Compute each source cell content width once during the width pass; keep vislen on the cached path; avoid novel strings that miss the cache

finding: stream() uses bounded-sample geometry; a wide post-sample ColSpan mis-sizes or tempts mid-stream width recompute which would break O(1)/row streaming (FUNCTIONAL_SPEC 11)
proposed_action: Make rule explicit: keep sample geometry, wrap/clip post-sample spans, forbid mid-stream _compute_cols_width; document limitation

finding: No rendering benchmark exists in repo or plan; performance guarantees are unprovable per the performance lens
evidence: no benchmarks/ dir; colspan plan 4 (functional tests only)
proposed_action: Add dependency-free benchmarks/bench_render.py; capture baseline on main before colspan; re-run after

finding: _span_block_width sums a list slice self._width[start:start+colspan]; slice alloc is negligible unless called per display line (compounds with P1)
proposed_action: Sum without slicing on any per-line path; acceptable as-is off the per-line path
